description: ClawHQ backlog — derived from PRODUCT.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, README.md (2026-03-18)

items.title: Create docs/CONFIGURATION.md — blueprint schema, skill schema, every config option
items.intent: Users writing custom blueprints or skills must have a single reference document — reading 7 example YAMLs and reverse-engineering the schema is not documentation.
items.description: Configuration content exists but only as examples and scattered references: configs/blueprints/*.yaml (7 blueprints establishing the blueprint schema by example), configs/skills/*/config.yaml (6 skills establishing the skill schema by example), configs/skills/*/SKILL.md (6 behavior definitions establishing the SKILL.md format by example), docs/ARCHITECTURE.md ('What a Blueprint Configures' 10-dimension table, 'The 14 Landmine Rules' table, 'What Gets Generated' directory tree), docs/OPENCLAW-REFERENCE.md (openclaw.json reference section 10, Configuration Surface Inventory section 11, The 14 Configuration Landmines section 12 with detailed explanations, Automation section 14 for cron config), CLAUDE.md (Key Design Constraints, CLI Commands). The unified document must cover: blueprint YAML schema (field-by-field, every dimension from the 10-dimension table), skill config.yaml schema (field-by-field: schedule, model, dependencies, approval, boundaries), SKILL.md authoring format, the 14 landmine rules with explanations, model routing configuration, and integration configuration patterns.
steps: Read configs/blueprints/email-manager.yaml and configs/blueprints/research-copilot.yaml as reference blueprints to extract the full field set
Read configs/skills/email-digest/config.yaml and configs/skills/morning-brief/config.yaml as reference skills to extract the full field set
Read configs/skills/email-digest/SKILL.md as reference for the SKILL.md authoring format
Read docs/ARCHITECTURE.md sections: 'What a Blueprint Configures' (10-dimension table), 'The 14 Landmine Rules', 'What Gets Generated'
Read docs/OPENCLAW-REFERENCE.md sections 10 (openclaw.json), 11 (Configuration Surface Inventory), 12 (14 Configuration Landmines with detailed explanations), 14 (cron/heartbeat/hooks)
Read CLAUDE.md Key Design Constraints section

Compose docs/CONFIGURATION.md with sections: Blueprint Schema (every field, type, required/optional, description, default), Skill Schema (config.yaml field-by-field + SKILL.md format guide), The 14 Landmine Rules (sourced from OPENCLAW-REFERENCE.md section 12, each rule with explanation and safe default), Model Routing (local vs cloud, per-task-type routing), Integration Patterns (how to configure IMAP, SMTP, CalDAV, etc.), Generated Directory Structure (from ARCHITECTURE.md 'What Gets Generated')
For each schema field, document: name, type, required/optional, default, description, example value
acceptanceCriteria: docs/CONFIGURATION.md exists and documents the full blueprint YAML schema, skill config.yaml schema, SKILL.md format, and all 14 landmine rules
Every blueprint schema field includes: name, type, required/optional, default, description
Every skill config.yaml field includes: name, type, required/optional, default, description
The 14 landmine rules each have an explanation and the safe default ClawHQ enforces
A user can write a custom blueprint or skill using only this document as reference
items.groomingNotes: PO PASS (2026-03-23). AC REWRITTEN — prior AC demanded skill config.yaml schema, SKILL.md format, and 14 landmine rules which the steps (blueprint-only scope, Tech Lead Pass 3) cannot produce. Root cause of failCount=1: AC and steps were misaligned, so validation always failed on skill/landmine criteria even when blueprint schema was correct. Fix: AC now matches the fallback scope exactly — blueprint YAML schema + Generated Directory Structure only. Intent narrowed accordingly. FOLLOW-UP NEEDED: skill config.yaml schema, SKILL.md format, and 14 landmine rules belong in a separate FEAT once blueprint section ships cleanly. Do not expand scope of this item again.

items.title: Slim README.md to storefront format — problem, solution, quickstart, links
items.intent: The README must sell the project in 60 seconds — the current README is a product document pretending to be a landing page.
items.description: The current README.md is a comprehensive product document containing architecture diagrams, deployment tables, data sovereignty matrices, CLI command listings, and blueprint dimension tables. This content belongs in docs/ files (most of which already exist in docs/ARCHITECTURE.md, docs/PRODUCT.md, docs/OPENCLAW-REFERENCE.md, or will be created by FEAT-006 through FEAT-012). After the docs/ files exist, the README must be cut to storefront format: one-sentence pitch ('WordPress for AI agents'), the problem (13,500 tokens, 14 landmines, abandoned deployments — 3 sentences max from the existing second paragraph), the solution (what ClawHQ does — 1 paragraph), quick start (install + init — 3 commands max, link to docs/QUICKSTART.md), the blueprint concept (1 paragraph + simple example, link to docs/CONFIGURATION.md), status (honest assessment), links section (all docs/ files), author section. Remove: How It Works detail, The Platform CLI listings, Blueprints dimension table, Cloud section, Deployment Options table, Data Sovereignty table, Why This Exists (fold into problem/solution). All removed content must have a home in a docs/ file.
steps: Verify that docs/ARCHITECTURE.md (with FEAT-006 skill section), docs/QUICKSTART.md, docs/CONFIGURATION.md, docs/PROBLEMS.md, docs/ROADMAP.md, docs/CONTRIBUTING.md, and docs/CHANGELOG.md all exist
Keep in README.md: opening pitch ('WordPress for AI agents'), the problem paragraph (13,500 tokens, 14 landmines, abandoned deployments), the solution paragraph ('ClawHQ turns generic...')
Keep in README.md: the three-layer ASCII diagram from How It Works (Cloud/Blueprints/Platform) but remove all detail below it
Add abbreviated Quick Start: install one-liner, clawhq init --guided, clawhq up — then link to docs/QUICKSTART.md
Add one blueprint example (email-manager in 3 lines) then link to docs/CONFIGURATION.md
Add Status section with honest assessment
Add Links section listing every docs/ file with one-line description
Add Author section: Simon Plant, building AI infrastructure tools, open to roles
Remove: The Platform (CLI listings), Blueprints (dimension table, evolution commands), Cloud (managed hosting details), Deployment Options table, Data Sovereignty table, Why This Exists section
Verify all removed content exists in a docs/ file — nothing lost
acceptanceCriteria: README.md contains only: pitch, problem, solution, three-layer diagram, quick start (abbreviated), one blueprint example, status, links, author
Every section removed from README.md has a corresponding docs/ file containing the content
Links section includes: Architecture, Quickstart, Configuration, Problems, Roadmap, Contributing, Changelog
No content is lost — everything is in README.md or linked from it
items.groomingNotes: RE-GROOMED 2026-03-23. Sprint record confirms: FEAT-006, FEAT-007, FEAT-009, FEAT-010, FEAT-011, FEAT-012 all complete. Sole remaining blocker: FEAT-008 (CONFIGURATION.md). When FEAT-008 ships, mark readyForSprint=true immediately — no further grooming needed. AC is tight: 150-line limit, content audit, no lost content. Priority must is correct.

items.title: Add .doc-standard.yaml schema file for doc structure enforcement
items.intent: The repo must declare its doc standard version and structure so automated checks can validate compliance — undeclared standards are unenforceable.
items.description: Part of a cross-repo documentation standard shared with aishore, GigOS, and other portfolio repos. The .doc-standard.yaml declares: schema version, project metadata (name: ClawHQ, tagline: WordPress for AI agents, category: infrastructure), required files list matching the docs/ structure, cross-repo links to sibling projects (aishore, Easy-Markdown, GigOS), and author info for consistency enforcement across the portfolio. This file enables a future GitHub Action to validate doc structure compliance on PR. Note: ClawHQ has additional docs not in the standard (docs/PRODUCT.md, docs/OPENCLAW-REFERENCE.md) — these should be listed in an optional_files section so the validator doesn't flag them as non-standard.
steps: Create .doc-standard.yaml at repo root
Set schema_version to '1.0'
Set project name to 'ClawHQ', tagline to 'WordPress for AI agents', category to 'infrastructure'
List required_files: README.md, docs/ARCHITECTURE.md, docs/QUICKSTART.md, docs/CONFIGURATION.md, docs/PROBLEMS.md, docs/ROADMAP.md, docs/CONTRIBUTING.md, docs/CHANGELOG.md
Add optional_files: docs/PRODUCT.md, docs/OPENCLAW-REFERENCE.md (repo-specific docs not in the standard)
Add portfolio section with sibling repos: aishore (complementary), Easy-Markdown (related), GigOS (related)
Add author section: name Simon Plant, location Santa Barbara CA, status 'Building AI infrastructure tools. Open to roles.'
Verify the file is valid YAML
acceptanceCriteria: .doc-standard.yaml exists at repo root and is valid YAML
File declares schema_version, project metadata, required_files, optional_files, portfolio siblings, and author info
required_files matches the docs/ structure created by FEAT-006 through FEAT-012
optional_files lists docs/PRODUCT.md and docs/OPENCLAW-REFERENCE.md so they are not flagged as non-standard
Portfolio section lists aishore, Easy-Markdown, and GigOS with correct GitHub URLs
items.groomingNotes: GROOMED 2026-03-23. Blocked by FEAT-013 (must, not yet started). Steps are prescriptive and complete — no ambiguity on what to produce. AC is entirely objective (valid YAML, specific fields, specific URLs). Priority could is correct: this is a cross-repo consistency mechanism, not user-facing. When FEAT-013 ships, this can be picked up immediately — single file, no logic, fast sprint.

items.title: Extend docs/CONFIGURATION.md — skill config.yaml schema, SKILL.md format, 14 landmine rules
items.intent: Users writing custom skills and troubleshooting config errors must have a complete reference — the skill schema, SKILL.md authoring format, and 14 landmine rules must be documented in CONFIGURATION.md alongside the blueprint schema.
items.description: docs/CONFIGURATION.md was scoped to blueprint YAML schema only (FEAT-008 fallback). This item extends it with the three remaining sections: skill config.yaml schema (field-by-field: schedule, model, dependencies, approval, boundaries), SKILL.md authoring format (the behavior definition format used in configs/skills/*/SKILL.md), and the 14 configuration landmines (each with explanation and the safe default ClawHQ enforces). Source material: configs/skills/*/config.yaml (6 examples), configs/skills/*/SKILL.md (6 examples), docs/OPENCLAW-REFERENCE.md sections 10-12 (Configuration Surface Inventory, 14 Landmines), docs/ARCHITECTURE.md ('The 14 Landmine Rules' table). This completes the original FEAT-008 intent — a user can write a custom blueprint OR skill using only CONFIGURATION.md.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/CONFIGURATION.md contains a Skill Config Schema section documenting every field in configs/skills/*/config.yaml: schedule, model, dependencies, approval, boundaries — each with name, type, required/optional, default, description
docs/CONFIGURATION.md contains a SKILL.md Authoring Format section documenting the structure and required fields of configs/skills/*/SKILL.md files
docs/CONFIGURATION.md contains a Configuration Landmines section listing all 14 rules, each with: the landmine name, what goes wrong if violated, and the safe default ClawHQ enforces
A user can write a custom skill using only docs/CONFIGURATION.md — no other source file needed
